knimeNode.name: Spark Expression (Hyim)
knimeNode.shortDescription: Applies multiple Spark SQL expressions to transform or add columns in a Spark DataFrame.

intro.p: This node applies one or more Spark SQL expressions to transform or add columns in a Spark DataFrame. Each expression row specifies:

ul.li: Expression: A Spark SQL expression (e.g., UPPER(col1), col1 + col2, CAST(col1 AS INT))
Output Mode: Whether to APPEND a new column or REPLACE an existing one
Output Column Name: The name of the output column

intro.p: Expressions are applied sequentially using Spark's withColumn() API, so later expressions can reference columns created by earlier expressions.

ul.li: CASE WHEN age > 18 THEN 'adult' ELSE 'minor' END — conditional

intro.p: Use backticks for column names that contain spaces or special characters: `my column`

tab.name: Expression
tab.option: Left panel (top): Lists all columns from the input Spark DataFrame with their data types. Double-click a column name to insert it into the currently active expression editor. Column names with spaces or special characters are automatically wrapped in backticks.

Left panel (bottom): A categorized list of common Spark SQL functions (String, Math, Date/Time, Null Handling, Type Cast, Conditional). Double-click a function to insert its template into the active expression editor.

Center area: Each tab represents one expression transformation. Use the + button to add a new expression and - to remove the current one. Expressions are applied sequentially, so later expressions can reference columns created by earlier ones.
The main text area in each tab where you write a Spark SQL expression (e.g., UPPER(col1), col1 + col2). All standard Spark SQL functions are supported. Reference column names directly.

APPEND: Creates a new column with the specified name. The column name must not already exist in the input. REPLACE: Replaces an existing column with the expression result. The column name must exist in the input.
The name of the output column. For APPEND mode, this must be a new name. For REPLACE mode, this must match an existing column name.

Bottom area: Click Evaluate to validate all expressions by running them on the Spark cluster (LIMIT 5 rows). Shows a formatted preview of the result on success, or an error message on failure. The upstream node must be executed for evaluation to work.
